everyone.name: Lisa-Marie Namphy
everyone.bio: Lisa is a developer advocate and community architect, and is Head of Developer Relations at Intuit (home of Argo Proj). Lisa is also a CNCF Ambassador and Silicon Valley Cloud Native User Group organizer (with over 7000 members). Lisa is an advocate and frequent speaker for open source technology and Diversity, Equity & Inclusion initiatives, a writer, an avid sports fan, and loves wine and dogs. Follow Lisa on Twitter @SWDevAngel and LinkedIn @lnamphy.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: It has been a great pleasure to be a CNCF Ambassador for the past several years and I am thrilled to continue the journey. I love working with this particular group of colleagues to evangelize open source and cloud native technology, to mentor new community members, to give a platform and a voice and to encourage participation in ALL areas of the cloud native ecosystem. And to have the support of the CNCF to extend the reach of cloud native technology to "the last mile" has been an extremely rewarding experience that I am grateful and excited to continue! :) Looking so much forward to working with all of you in the coming years! :)

everyone.name: Saim Safdar
everyone.bio: Hello, my name is Saim Safdar, working as a Developer Relations Manager at Rafay Systems, host of the cloud-native podcast (https://www.youtube.com/@cloudnativefm), co-founder of Cloud Native Islamabad (https://community.cncf.io/islamabad/), Organizer of KCD Pakistan (https://www.cncf.io/blog/2023/03/10/from-community-group-to-event-a-kcd-pakistan-experience/). Maintainer of the cncf Sandbox project, Paralus (https://www.paralus.io/).Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I love sharing my Open Source jourey with others, currently there's a desparate need of mentorship within the community, as cloud native eco-system constantly equipped with new tooling, students, tech pro, difficult to find and map existing knowledge to work with OS and communities, I'm hoping my time as an Ambassador, I'll get on-board and shortened the loop for code to contribution to outreaching new OS tooling to the world.

everyone.name: Jacopo Nardiello
everyone.bio: Founder at SIGHUP, I have been involved with Kubernetes and the CNCF since its inception in 2015. I am passionate about Kubernetes and full lifecycle management of production-grade infrastructures, open standards and currently serving in the advisory board of Linux Foundation Europe.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to continue supporting the CNCF, both as a community of like-minded individuals and as a technological ecosystem. My mission is to encourage organizations to adopt open source projects as a foundation for the evolution of their infrastructure and governance, promoting the use of OSS and Open Standards.

everyone.name: Janelle Dougherty
everyone.bio: I've been a devops engineer for the last seven years. I love building, automating, and troubleshooting infrastructure across diverse environments. I'm passionate about all things kubernetes and cloud-native. And, absolutely love tinkering with things even though this usually means they get broken.

everyone.name: Dominik Steiner
everyone.bio: As an infrastructure specialist, I architect robust enterprise data center solutions using the VMware Cloud Foundation stack (vSphere, vSAN, NSX, Tanzu) and Cisco technologies (UCS, Nexus, ACI), while driving modernization through Kubernetes and GitOps. Alongside my technical work, I serve as a lecturer in the Master’s program at the University of Applied Sciences St. Pölten, bringing real-world enterprise insights directly to the next generation of IT professionals.

everyone.name: Mohamed Hedi Lajili
everyone.bio: I'm a data engineer deeply passionate about building trustworthy data ecosystems through robust governance and automated ETL processes. My journey to becoming a Kubestronaut represents my commitment to mastering cloud-native technologies and applying them to create scalable, high-performance data solutions. I specialize in designing pipelines that not only move data efficiently but also ensure its quality and reliability. I'm always excited to connect with fellow professionals to share insights and explore innovative approaches to data integration challenges.

ambassadors.name: Lisa-Marie Namphy
ambassadors.bio: Lisa is a developer advocate and community architect, and is Head of Developer Relations at Intuit (home of Argo Proj). Lisa is also a CNCF Ambassador and Silicon Valley Cloud Native User Group organizer (with over 7000 members). Lisa is an advocate and frequent speaker for open source technology and Diversity, Equity & Inclusion initiatives, a writer, an avid sports fan, and loves wine and dogs. Follow Lisa on Twitter @SWDevAngel and LinkedIn @lnamphy.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: It has been a great pleasure to be a CNCF Ambassador for the past several years and I am thrilled to continue the journey. I love working with this particular group of colleagues to evangelize open source and cloud native technology, to mentor new community members, to give a platform and a voice and to encourage participation in ALL areas of the cloud native ecosystem. And to have the support of the CNCF to extend the reach of cloud native technology to "the last mile" has been an extremely rewarding experience that I am grateful and excited to continue! :) Looking so much forward to working with all of you in the coming years! :)

ambassadors.name: Qing Hao
ambassadors.bio: Qing Hao is a senior software engineer at Red Hat. She loves open source and sharing, she is a maintainer of Open Cluster Management and enjoys writing blogs to share new developments in the community. She has been involved in numerous open source activities over the past few years, including being a speaker at KubeCon, a mentor for OSPP and GSoC many times. She has hosted many open source meetups in Xi’an as early as 2015, and she is now one of the organizers of KCD.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am passionate about open source and sharing, and becoming an ambassador would provide me with more opportunities and motivation to do what I love. I’ve witnessed the CNCF project’s remarkable growth in recent years, and now, with the rise of AI, it’s thriving more than ever. As an ambassador, I look forward to helping more people and projects benefit from open source, which is one of the most exciting aspects of this role.

ambassadors.name: Spas Atanasov
ambassadors.bio: Spas Atanasov is a Manager of Site Reliability Engineering at MariaDB with more than 10 years of experience working with Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. He is a Co-organizer of the Cloud Native Meetups in Bulgaria since 2018, where the community has grown from 20 to over 2000 members, and a Co-organizer of Kubernetes Community Days Sofia. He speaks at meetups and actively mentors people, helping them gain the confidence to step on stage and share their knowledge. His focus areas include Kubernetes at scale, SRE, Cloud Native, Observability, and Cost Optimization. He strongly believes in the power of community and the idea that sharing knowledge can change the way people think and work. Based in Sofia, Bulgaria, Spas enjoys baking sourdough bread, making barbecues, and cooking huge stacks of pancakes – but most of all, spending time with his family. He is also known for his favorite question: "Why?". Why he is motivated: "I love seeing the spark of satisfaction in people's eyes when I have helped them learn something or see things from a different perspective."

ambassadors.name: Thomas Labarussias
ambassadors.bio: I'm OSS/Ecosystem Advocate at Sysdig, the company which created and open-sourced Falco, the Security Runtime Engine for Kubernetes and Cloud-Native technologies. I worked for Qonto, a modern banking for SMEs and freelancers, where I managed their Kubernetes clusters and the enthusiastic tools around, like ArgoCD, Traefik, Prometheus. I also assisted for many years pure-players and e-business companies for a large managed service provider, as an AWS expert and FinOps. I'm one of the longest tenured members of the Falco community, and the creator of Falcosidekick and Falcosidekick-UI, two major components of the Falco ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As an SRE for several years, I've been able to use a number of CNCF projects in production. I'm also one of the maintainers of the most advanced OSS security project in the landscape. I would like to help even more architects and users to integrate these projects into their infrastructures. Moreover, in 2024, the KubeCon EU will be in Paris, France, it's important for me to do my best to help the organizers and show the vitality of French tech.

ambassadors.name: Husni Alhamdani
ambassadors.bio: Husni is a CNCF Ambassador and a Sr. Site Reliability Engineer, where he is responsible for building and maintaining infrastructure platforms. In addition to these responsibilities, he primarily focuses on architecting Cloud-Native solutions. He also graduated from the LFX mentorship Program in 2022 (Kyverno Project) and in spare time, contributed to some CNCF Project like Kubernetes, OpenTelemetry, Istio, Cilium, KEDA.  He also founded Kubenesia (Indonesia's #1 Kubernetes & Cloud-Native Training Specialist) where he teach Kubernetes & Cloud-Native Technology.

ambassadors.name: Fabrizio Sgura
ambassadors.bio: Committed to innovation and new technologies, strong believer in cloud native and container orchestration. Participating in main events, both as speaker and attendant, actively spreading the CNCF principles and project. My job is my favorite game.

ambassadors.name: Jakub Scholz
ambassadors.bio: Jakub has a long experience with message-oriented middleware and currently focuses mainly on Apache Kafka and its integration with Kubernetes. He is one of the founders and maintainers of the Strimzi project. Strimzi provides operators and other tools to make running Apache Kafka on Kubernetes as easy as possible. He also likes to talk about anything related to Apache Kafka and Kubernetes.

ambassadors.name: Toru Komatsu
ambassadors.bio: Toru contributes to OSS as an individual contributor. He is the author and lead developer of youki, an OCI Runtime in Rust, and I am also a maintainer of the OCI Runtime Specification. Additionally, he serves as a reviewer for runwasi and is involved in developing a world that utilizes containers and Wasm. Lately, he has developed an interest in Kubernetes schedulers. Furthermore, he has given presentations at KubeDay Japan and has spoken at various community events in Japan.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The area of container runtime is the unsung hero of the Cloud Native space, where the intricacies of the technology and new features often don't get the spotlight they deserve. As a developer in this field, I want to help more people become aware of the exciting features it has to offer. I aim to encourage them to take the leap into this fascinating domain! I am also looking forward to assisting the Japanese Cloud Native community in becoming more vibrant on an international level.

kubestronauts.name: Janelle Dougherty
kubestronauts.bio: I've been a devops engineer for the last seven years. I love building, automating, and troubleshooting infrastructure across diverse environments. I'm passionate about all things kubernetes and cloud-native. And, absolutely love tinkering with things even though this usually means they get broken.

kubestronauts.name: Gaber Senan
kubestronauts.bio: Over 10+ years of experience in software using different technologies with solid experience in integration and middleware systems development. I gained experience in building multi tiers applications and enterprise level systems integration in different fields payment, government sectors. I participated in full system development life cycle .

kubestronauts.name: Shar Qureshi
kubestronauts.bio: I am a Partner in PwC’s Digital Assurance and Transparency practice based in NY. In financial services for over 23 years and the last decade has been focused towards controls advisory, assurance and security engagements for workloads hosted using cloud and containerized environments

kubestronauts.name: Oscar Wieman
kubestronauts.bio: I’m a Platform and DevSecOps Engineer with a passion for cloud-native technologies and open source. I enjoy tinkering with my Kubernetes cluster and ArgoCD, constantly learning and contributing to projects while also building up my Golang skills. Outside of work, I live with my girlfriend and our cat, and I’m into gaming and multiple sports.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Lavanya Anbalagan
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am an enthusiastic learner with over five years of experience in enterprise container platforms and holds a Kubestronaut title. In addition to my professional achievements, I have a personal interest in exploring new places and maintaining physical fitness.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Oleksandr Anosov
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm a software engineer with a strong background in backend development, cloud-native technologies, and performance optimization. With experience in C++, Python, Go, and Rust, I’ve worked on data-intensive applications, cloud infrastructure, and FinOps solutions. As an active member of the Ukrainian Rust community, I’m passionate about functional programming, Kubernetes, and cost-efficient cloud computing. Holding multiple Kubernetes and AWS certifications, I continuously explore new technologies to build scalable and resilient systems.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Subhash Polisetti
goldenKubestronauts.bio: While most people dream of electric sheep, I ponder about the tech~no~logical singularity while orchestrating containers [aka tesseracts], debugging AI infrastructure whilst not summoning any basilisks to bootload humanity up the Kardashev scale. I occasionally transmit thoughts about building things at log.101010.dev (whenever I escape the event horizon of my todo list). For more mysterious adven-char[c]s from this localhost, resolve my hostname at port 42 (ARPANET~style) on 127001.page ⏳

goldenKubestronauts.name: Olivier Calzi
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Olivier Calzi is a Site Reliability Engineer (Linux) and freelance IT Manager / Cloud Architect, with deep specialization in CNCF technologies and modern cloud-native infrastructure. He brings hands-on expertise in production-grade Kubernetes environments, with a strong focus on platform resilience, security, and DevOps culture. With experience across both startup and enterprise ecosystems, Olivier contributes actively to the Cloud Native community through mentoring, technical writing, and open collaboration. He is passionate about building robust, scalable systems and continuously advancing the state of cloud operations.
